Details: Ozone is effective for the removal of  acetochlor degradates in drinking water applications (ESA, OXA, hydroxyacetochlor, deschloroacetochlor, 2-chloro-2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide, 2-hydroxy-2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide, 2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide, and 2-ethyl-6-methylaniline). Two studies, one bench-scale and one full-scale, found typical removals between 55 and 100 percent. Studies stated that due to the cleaving of double bonds and the opening of aromatic rings, alcohols, carbonyls, and carboxylic acids are created during ozonation.
